title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
summary: Victor Savkin critiques coding agents for lacking persistent context and memory across interactions, limiting their effectiveness despite individual capability. He frames this as analogous to a perfect engineer with amnesia and proposes Nx and Polygraph as frameworks to address this constraint through better codebase visibility and agent autonomy.
writeup: Victor Savkin argues that coding agents are individually capable but lack persistent context and memory across interactions, a gap he compares to a skilled engineer with amnesia. The talk frames this missing memory as the main constraint on agent effectiveness rather than raw model capability. It presents Nx and Polygraph as frameworks meant to address the limitation through better codebase visibility and greater agent autonomy.
